# OwlSOC

> OwlSOC is an AI-powered Security Operations Center (SOC) service that
> investigates security alerts from Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Defender and
> AWS. When an alert fires, OwlSOC pulls the relevant logs, builds an
> evidence-linked timeline, maps the attack to MITRE ATT&CK, and returns a
> plain-language verdict with a recommended action, typically in under two
> minutes, 24/7. A human on the customer's team approves any action before it
> runs. In practice it automates alert triage for Microsoft Sentinel, Defender
> and AWS: every alert is triaged and fully investigated rather than sampled.

## Key facts

- Connects to: Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Defender (Endpoint and Office), and
  AWS Security Hub at launch; other sources (e.g. AWS GuardDuty, Entra ID) scoped
  on request. Read-only by default; no agents to install; nothing in the traffic
  path.
- Speed: every alert is triaged automatically, and AI investigation typically
  completes in under two minutes, around the clock.
- Human-in-the-loop: OwlSOC investigates and recommends; a human approves every
  action, and execution is gated by the write scopes the customer grants. All
  actions are logged; reversible actions can be undone, and the few that cannot
  be reversed are flagged before approval.
- Output per investigation: plain-language verdict with confidence,
  evidence-linked timeline (every claim cites the source log), MITRE ATT&CK
  mapping, affected entities, recommended actions.
- Pricing (per monitored environment, monthly, no minimum term):
  Standalone £495/mo · Core £1,495/mo · Compliance £2,995/mo.
- Pilot: £495, 30 days, fully refundable on request, one environment, typically
  live within 48 hours of the OAuth grant.
- Data handling: UK/EU data residency; encrypted in transit and at rest; no
  model training on customer data (contractual commitment across all
  sub-processors); DPA available on request.
- Validation: each release is tested against a fixed set of real-world attack
  scenarios (AiTM token theft, encoded PowerShell, OAuth consent abuse, mass
  exfiltration, and others) and must reach the expected verdict, with the sourced
  timeline behind it, on that set. This is a defined test set, not a guarantee about any
  specific environment; the refundable pilot exists so buyers can verify on
  their own alerts.
- OwlSOC is an early-stage, founder-led team; formal company and contract details are provided before any paid pilot begins.
- The demo on the website uses synthetic, clearly-labelled illustrative data,
  never real customer data.

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.owlsoc.com/): product, how it works, interactive sample
  investigation, pricing, FAQ, pilot sign-up
- [What is an AI SOC?](https://www.owlsoc.com/ai-soc/): definition of an AI SOC,
  how it works, and how it differs from a traditional SOC, MDR, and automation
- [AI SOC vs MDR](https://www.owlsoc.com/ai-soc-vs-mdr/): how an AI SOC compares
  to managed detection and response
- [AI SOC vs hiring a SOC team](https://www.owlsoc.com/ai-soc-vs-soc-team/):
  build-vs-buy comparison against an in-house 24/7 SOC
- [Microsoft Sentinel alert triage](https://www.owlsoc.com/microsoft-sentinel/):
  automating Microsoft Sentinel alert investigation
- [Microsoft Defender investigation](https://www.owlsoc.com/microsoft-defender/):
  automating Microsoft Defender (Endpoint/Office) alert investigation
- [AWS Security Hub triage](https://www.owlsoc.com/aws-security-hub/):
  investigating AWS Security Hub findings
- [Privacy policy](https://www.owlsoc.com/privacy/)
- [Terms of service](https://www.owlsoc.com/terms/)

## Contact

- General enquiries, security disclosures, and privacy / DPA requests: info@owlsoc.com
  (security contact also at /.well-known/security.txt)
